Class AsyncClient

java.lang.Object
com.client.network.AsyncClient
public class AsyncClient extends Object
Non-blocking TCP client for connecting to the server. This class handles: - Connecting to the server asynchronously - Sending requests and receiving responses - Managing the connection lifecycle - Reconnecting when server becomes unavailable Uses Java NIO for non-blocking I/O, which means operations like connect(), read(), and write() return immediately and complete asynchronously. Usage: AsyncClient client = new AsyncClient("localhost", 8080); client.connect(); Response response = client.send(request); client.disconnect();

    AsyncClient(String host, int port)
    Creates a new client that will connect to the specified server.

  • Constructor Details

    • AsyncClient

      public AsyncClient(String host, int port)
      Creates a new client that will connect to the specified server.
      Parameters:
      host - Server hostname or IP (e.g., "localhost" or "192.168.1.1")
      port - Server port number (e.g., 8080)

  • Method Details

    • connect

      public boolean connect() throws IOException
      Connects to the server. This is a non-blocking operation - it returns immediately and the actual connection happens in the background.
      Returns:
      true if connection succeeded
      Throws:
      IOException - If connection fails

    • reconnect

      public boolean reconnect() throws IOException
      Attempts to reconnect to the server. Tries to reconnect up to MAX_RECONNECT_ATTEMPTS times with delays between attempts.
      Returns:
      true if reconnection succeeded
      Throws:
      IOException - If all reconnection attempts fail

    • reconnect

      public boolean reconnect(int attempts) throws IOException
      Attempts to reconnect to the server with specified number of attempts.
      Parameters:
      attempts - Number of reconnection attempts
      Returns:
      true if reconnection succeeded
      Throws:
      IOException - If all reconnection attempts fail

    • send

      public Response send(Request request) throws IOException
      Sends a request to the server and waits for response. This method: 1. Serializes the request to bytes 2. Writes bytes to server 3. Reads response bytes from server 4. Deserializes response
      Parameters:
      request - The request to send
      Returns:
      Response from server
      Throws:
      IOException - If send/receive fails

    • sendWithRetry

      public Response sendWithRetry(Request request) throws IOException
      Attempts to send a request with automatic reconnection on failure. If the first attempt fails, tries to reconnect and retry once.
      Parameters:
      request - The request to send
      Returns:
      Response from server
      Throws:
      IOException - If both attempts fail

    • disconnect

      public void disconnect()
      Disconnects from the server. Closes the channel if it's open.

    • isConnected

      public boolean isConnected()
      Checks if client is currently connected to server.
      Returns:
      true if connected and channel is open

    • getHost

      public String getHost()
      Gets the server hostname.
      Returns:
      The host

    • getPort

      public int getPort()
      Gets the server port.
      Returns:
      The port
